comment_text.sn22.85:1.3: He equates the Buddha’s teaching of cessation with annihilation. Not all annihilationists believe that there is only one lifetime. Some, for example, believed that there is an allotted course of lives, perhaps predetermined, at the end of which all beings come to an end, as “transmigration is limited” (pariyantakate saṁsāre, DN 2:20.9, SN 24.8:1.13). Such was the doctrine taught by the Bamboo-staffed Ascetic Gosāla to the Ājīvakas.
comment_text.sn22.85:8.2: “Realized one” (tathāgata) commonly refers to the Buddha himself, but it may also refer to any arahant. In this and similar passages, the nature of the arahant is discussed, and it does not directly bear on the Buddha specifically, so I take it as referring to any arahant. | This passage relies on the label usage of the nominative, as indicated by the particle -ti. It is a tricky passage to render consistently and coherently, so I make the syntax explicit.
comment_text.sn22.85:10.2: This passage occurs also at SN 22.86:10.2 and SN 44.2:17.5. As it stands in the MS edition, it appears to repeat the first set of propositions (rūpaṁ tathāgatoti samanupassasi). The commentary reads all as nominative, and says it means lumping all the aggregates together, which is followed by most modern translations. However, Pali editions mostly punctuate as if these were individual items, and this is supported by the parallel at SA 104. Pali readings vary widely, but in most cases, the negative passage just below uses the possessive forms, whereas the current phrase (apart from a few variants) does not use the possessive forms. This leaves a gap in the analysis, since possession is one of the standard relationships with the aggregates. I propose that the passage is corrupt, and read as individual items implying possession. This suggestion claims a little support in that the possessive rupī is found in the Thai Royal edition (vol. 17, p. 99), although the remaining items differ.
comment_text.sn22.85:11.2: The construction ayaṁ so (“that one”) specifies an aforementioned individual (SN 1.20:7.3, AN 6.16:5.5, Kd 1:42.2.2). Here, this must be the subject of the previous passage, i.e. the one who was formerly considered “with form” and now “without form”.
comment_text.sn22.85:28.8: Some editions add an extra sentence indicating that Yamaka rejoiced in Sāriputta’s teaching.

root_text.sn22.85:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn22.85:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena yamakassa nāma bhikkhuno evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn22.85:1.3: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:2.1: Assosuṁ kho sambahulā bhikkhū yamakassa kira nāma bhikkhuno evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn22.85:2.2: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:2.3: Atha kho te bhikkhū yenāyasmā yamako tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā yamakena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn22.85:2.4: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ yamakaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:3.1: “Saccaṁ kira te, āvuso yamaka, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:3.2: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:3.3: “Evaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi:
root_text.sn22.85:3.4: ‘khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:4.1: “Mā, āvuso yamaka, evaṁ avaca, mā bhagavantaṁ abbhācikkhi. Na hi sādhu bhagavato abbhācikkhanaṁ. Na hi bhagavā evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn22.85:4.2: ‘khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:4.3: Evampi kho āyasmā yamako tehi bhikkhūhi vuccamāno tatheva taṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ thāmasā parāmāsā abhinivissa voharati:
root_text.sn22.85:4.4: “tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:5.1: Yato kho te bhikkhū nāsakkhiṁsu āyasmantaṁ yamakaṁ etasmā pāpakā diṭṭhigatā vivecetuṁ, atha kho te bhikkhū uṭṭhāyāsanā yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:5.2: “yamakassa nāma, āvuso sāriputta, bhikkhuno evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:5.3: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’ti.
root_text.sn22.85:5.4: Sādhāyasmā sāriputto yena yamako bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:5.6: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā yamako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā yamakena saddhiṁ sammodi …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ yamakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.85:6.1: “Saccaṁ kira te, āvuso yamaka, evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:6.2: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:6.3: “Evaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:10.2: rūpaṁ … vedanaṁ … saññaṁ … saṅkhāre … viññāṇaṁ tathāgatoti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:11.2: ayaṁ so arūpī … avedano … asaññī … asaṅkhāro … aviññāṇo tathāgatoti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:11.4: “Ettha ca te, āvuso yamaka, diṭṭheva dhamme saccato thetato tathāgate anupalabbhiyamāne, kallaṁ nu te taṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ:
root_text.sn22.85:11.5: ‘tathāhaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi, yathā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu kāyassa bhedā ucchijjati vinassati, na hoti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:12.1: “Ahu kho me taṁ, āvuso sāriputta, pubbe aviddasuno pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ;
root_text.sn22.85:12.2: idañca panāyasmato sāriputtassa dhammadesanaṁ sutvā tañceva pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ pahīnaṁ, dhammo ca me abhisamito”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:13.2: ‘yo so, āvuso yamaka, bhikkhu arahaṁ khīṇāsavo so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā kiṁ hotī’ti?
root_text.sn22.85:13.3: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, āvuso yamaka, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn22.85:13.5: ‘yo so, āvuso yamaka, bhikkhu arahaṁ khīṇāsavo so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā kiṁ hotī’ti?
root_text.sn22.85:14.2: Tena hāvuso yamaka, upamaṁ te karissāmi etasseva atthassa bhiyyoso mattāya ñāṇāya.
root_text.sn22.85:14.3: Seyyathāpi, āvuso yamaka, gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā aḍḍho mahaddhano mahābhogo;
root_text.sn22.85:14.5: Tassa kocideva puriso uppajjeyya anatthakāmo ahitakāmo ayogakkhemakāmo jīvitā voropetukāmo.
root_text.sn22.85:14.7: ‘ayaṁ kho gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā aḍḍho mahaddhano mahābhogo;
root_text.sn22.85:14.11: So taṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatiputtaṁ vā upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn22.85:14.13: Tamenaṁ so gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā upaṭṭhāpeyya.
root_text.sn22.85:14.14: So upaṭṭhaheyya pubbuṭṭhāyī pacchānipātī kiṅkārapaṭissāvī manāpacārī piyavādī.
root_text.sn22.85:14.15: Tassa so gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā mittatopi naṁ saddaheyya;
root_text.sn22.85:14.18: Yadā kho, āvuso, tassa purisassa evamassa:
root_text.sn22.85:14.19: ‘saṁvissattho kho myāyaṁ gahapati vā gahapatiputto vā’ti, atha naṁ rahogataṁ viditvā tiṇhena satthena jīvitā voropeyya.
root_text.sn22.85:15.2: yadā hi so puriso amuṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatiputtaṁ vā upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ āha:
root_text.sn22.85:15.6: Yadāpi so upaṭṭhahati pubbuṭṭhāyī pacchānipātī kiṅkārapaṭissāvī manāpacārī piyavādī, tadāpi so vadhakova.
root_text.sn22.85:15.9: Yadāpi naṁ rahogataṁ viditvā tiṇhena satthena jīvitā voropeti, tadāpi so vadhakova.
root_text.sn22.85:15.13: “Evameva kho, āvuso, assutavā puthujjano ariyānaṁ adassāvī ariyadhammassa akovido ariyadhamme avinīto, sappurisānaṁ adassāvī sappurisadhammassa akovido sappurisadhamme avinīto
root_text.sn22.85:15.14: rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.85:15.18: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.85:16.1: So aniccaṁ rūpaṁ ‘aniccaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.85:21.1: So rūpaṁ upeti upādiyati adhiṭṭhāti ‘attā me’ti.
root_text.sn22.85:21.6: Tassime pañcupādānakkhandhā upetā upādinnā dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.sn22.85:22.1: Sutavā ca kho, āvuso, ariyasāvako ariyānaṁ dassāvī …pe… sappurisadhamme suvinīto
root_text.sn22.85:22.2: na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ attānaṁ; na attani rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.85:22.6: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ attānaṁ;
root_text.sn22.85:22.7: na attani viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.85:23.1: So aniccaṁ rūpaṁ ‘aniccaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.85:28.1: So rūpaṁ na upeti, na upādiyati, nādhiṭṭhāti: ‘attā me’ti.
root_text.sn22.85:28.5: viññāṇaṁ na upeti, na upādiyati, nādhiṭṭhāti: ‘attā me’ti.
root_text.sn22.85:28.6: Tassime pañcupādānakkhandhā anupetā anupādinnā dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantī”ti.
root_text.sn22.85:28.7: “Evametaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, hoti yesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ tādisā sabrahmacārino anukampakā atthakāmā ovādakā anusāsakā.
root_text.sn22.85:28.8: Idañca pana me āyasmato sāriputtassa dhammadesanaṁ sutvā anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttan”ti.

translation_text.sn22.85:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn22.85:1.2: Now at that time a mendicant called Yamaka had the following harmful misconception:
translation_text.sn22.85:1.3: “As I understand the Buddha’s teaching, a mendicant who has ended the defilements is annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and doesn’t exist after death.”
translation_text.sn22.85:2.3: They went to Yamaka and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn22.85:2.4: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side and said to him,
translation_text.sn22.85:3.1: “Is it really true, Reverend Yamaka, that you have such a harmful misconception:
translation_text.sn22.85:3.2: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s teaching, a mendicant who has ended the defilements is annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and doesn’t exist after death’?”
translation_text.sn22.85:3.3: “Yes, reverends, that’s how I understand the Buddha’s teaching.”
translation_text.sn22.85:4.1: “Don’t say that, Yamaka! Don’t misrepresent the Buddha, for misrepresentation of the Buddha is not good. And the Buddha would not say that.”
translation_text.sn22.85:4.3: But even though admonished by those mendicants, Yamaka obstinately stuck to that misconception and insisted on stating it.
translation_text.sn22.85:5.1: When those mendicants were unable to dissuade Yamaka from that misconception, they got up from their seats and went to see Venerable Sāriputta. They told him what had happened, and said,
translation_text.sn22.85:5.4: “May Venerable Sāriputta please go to the mendicant Yamaka out of sympathy.”
translation_text.sn22.85:5.6: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Sāriputta came out of retreat, went to Venerable Yamaka and exchanged greetings with him. Seated to one side he said to Yamaka:
translation_text.sn22.85:6.1: “Is it really true, Reverend Yamaka, that you have such a harmful misconception:
translation_text.sn22.85:6.2: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s teaching, a mendicant who has ended the defilements is annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and doesn’t exist after death’?”
translation_text.sn22.85:6.3: “Yes, reverend, that’s how I understand the Buddha’s teaching.”
translation_text.sn22.85:7.11: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn22.85:8.1: What do you think, Reverend Yamaka?
translation_text.sn22.85:8.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to form?”
translation_text.sn22.85:8.4: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to feeling?” …
translation_text.sn22.85:9.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies in relation to form?”
translation_text.sn22.85:9.4: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies apart from form?”
translation_text.sn22.85:9.6: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies in relation to feeling?” …
translation_text.sn22.85:10.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to one who has form?” … “… feeling?” … “… perception?” … “… choices?” … “… consciousness?”
translation_text.sn22.85:11.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to that one who is without form?” … “… feeling?” … “… perception?” … “… choices?” … “… consciousness?”
translation_text.sn22.85:11.4: “In that case, Reverend Yamaka, since a realized one is not found by you as a genuine fact in this very life, is it cogent for you to declare:
translation_text.sn22.85:11.5: ‘As I understand the Buddha’s teaching, a mendicant who has ended the defilements is annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and doesn’t exist after death.’?”
translation_text.sn22.85:12.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, in my ignorance, I used to have that misconception.
translation_text.sn22.85:12.2: But now that I’ve heard the teaching from Venerable Sāriputta, I’ve given up that misconception, and I’ve comprehended the teaching.”
translation_text.sn22.85:13.1: “Reverend Yamaka, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn22.85:13.2: ‘When their body breaks up, after death, what happens to a perfected one, who has ended the defilements?’
translation_text.sn22.85:13.4: “Reverend, if they were to ask this,
translation_text.sn22.85:13.9: What’s suffering has ceased and disappeared.
translation_text.sn22.85:13.15: What’s suffering has ceased and disappeared.’
translation_text.sn22.85:13.16: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn22.85:14.2: Well then, I shall give you a simile to make the meaning even clearer.
translation_text.sn22.85:14.3: Suppose there was a householder or householder’s son who was rich, with a lot of money and great wealth,
translation_text.sn22.85:14.5: Then along comes a person who wants to harm, injure, and threaten him, and take his life.
translation_text.sn22.85:14.7: ‘This householder or householder’s son is rich, with a lot of money and great wealth,
translation_text.sn22.85:14.9: It won’t be easy to take his life by force.
translation_text.sn22.85:14.10: Why don’t I get close to him, then take his life?’
translation_text.sn22.85:14.11: So he goes up to that householder or householder’s son and says:
translation_text.sn22.85:14.13: They would serve that householder or householder’s son.
translation_text.sn22.85:14.14: They’d get up before him and go to bed after him, and be obliging, behaving nicely and speaking politely.
translation_text.sn22.85:14.15: The householder or householder’s son would consider them as a friend
translation_text.sn22.85:14.18: But when that person realizes that
translation_text.sn22.85:14.19: they’ve gained the trust of the householder or householder’s son, then, when they know he’s alone, they’d take his life with a sharp knife.
translation_text.sn22.85:15.2: When that person went to the householder or householder’s son and offered to
translation_text.sn22.85:15.3: serve him, weren’t they a killer then,
translation_text.sn22.85:15.6: And when they got up before him and went to bed after him, being obliging, behaving nicely and speaking politely, weren’t they a killer then,
translation_text.sn22.85:15.9: And when, knowing he was alone, they took his life with a sharp knife, weren’t they a killer then,
translation_text.sn22.85:15.13: “In the same way, an unlearned ordinary person has not seen the noble ones, and is neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve not seen true persons, and are neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.85:15.14: They regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.85:15.18: They regard consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.85:16.1: They don’t truly understand form—which is impermanent—as impermanent.
translation_text.sn22.85:17.1: They don’t truly understand form—which is suffering—as suffering.
translation_text.sn22.85:18.1: They don’t truly understand form—which is not-self—as not-self.
translation_text.sn22.85:19.1: They don’t truly understand form—which is conditioned—as conditioned.
translation_text.sn22.85:20.1: They don’t truly understand form—which is a killer—as a killer.
translation_text.sn22.85:20.5: consciousness—which is a killer—as a killer.
translation_text.sn22.85:21.1: They’re attracted to form, grasp it, and fixate on the thought, ‘my self’.
translation_text.sn22.85:21.5: consciousness, grasp it, and fixate on the thought, ‘my self’.
translation_text.sn22.85:21.6: And when you’ve gotten involved with and grasped these five grasping aggregates, they lead to your lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.sn22.85:22.1: A learned noble disciple has seen the noble ones, and is skilled and trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve seen true persons, and are skilled and trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.85:22.2: They don’t regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.85:22.6: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.85:22.7: consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.85:23.1: They truly understand form—which is impermanent—as impermanent.
translation_text.sn22.85:24.1: They truly understand form—which is suffering—as suffering.
translation_text.sn22.85:25.1: They truly understand form—which is not-self—as not-self.
translation_text.sn22.85:26.1: They truly understand form—which is conditioned—as conditioned.
translation_text.sn22.85:27.1: They truly understand form—which is a killer—as a killer.
translation_text.sn22.85:27.5: consciousness—which is a killer—as a killer.
translation_text.sn22.85:28.1: Not being attracted to form, they don’t grasp it, and fixate on the thought, ‘my self’.
translation_text.sn22.85:28.5: consciousness, they don't grasp it, and fixate on the thought, ‘my self’.
translation_text.sn22.85:28.6: And when you’re not attracted to and don’t grasp these five grasping aggregates, they lead to your lasting welfare and happiness.”
translation_text.sn22.85:28.7: “Reverend Sāriputta, this is how it is when you have such venerables as spiritual companions to advise and instruct you out of kindness and sympathy.
translation_text.sn22.85:28.8: And after hearing this teaching by Venerable Sāriputta, my mind is freed from the defilements by not grasping.”

variant_text.sn22.85:11.4: thetato → tathato (sya-all, km) | tathāgate anupalabbhiyamāne → tathāgato anupalabbhiyamāno (sya-all, pts1ed); tathāgate anupalabbhamāne (?)
variant_text.sn22.85:14.9: nāyaṁ sukaro → na vyāsukaro (bj); na hāyaṁ sukaro (sya-all, km)
